PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Continued To Apr-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: The Estate of Daniel. A. Lagan PES-15-299358 and The Daniel A. & Joanna A. Lagan Revocable Trust PTR-16-299549.

Appearances: Stephen Brooks for Debra "CeCe" Calli.

Mr. Brooks stated that Attorneys Jeffrey Lowe and Sarah Sheppard agree to a continuance, that the only thing remaining from their side is the issue of the US Savings Bonds and that they are working with the Treasury. The Petition for Letters of Administration, Petition for Instructions, and Complaint for Conversion After Rejection of Creditor's Claim are continued to April 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Continued To May-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Wall & Stephen Brooks for (petitioner) Debra "Cece" Calli. Jeffrey Loew for (respondent) Kamala Murphey.

Mr. Brooks stated that the decedent died intestate, that he revoked his will and trust, that petitioner had been named as Power of Attorney and agent, that she is the closest blood relative (niece), that there are significant assets outside of the revoked trust, that a sur-reply was received but not sure if it was filed, that it is speculation and hearsay, that it is not under penalty of perjury, that they should be able to respond to the sur-reply and that there was no court order authorizing a sur-reply. Mr. Loew stated that there is no effective revocation of the trust, that the petitioner has transferred assets to herself, and that there are conflicts that should be examined. The Court asked if there should be a separate action regarding the trust. Mr. Loew stated that the examiner suggested that a separate trust proceeding be filed. Mr. Wall stated that the issue of testate or intestate should first be considered by the court, that there are serious accounting issues, that items have been taken out of the grandfather's trust, that there is co-mingling, and that they would like a referral to mediation. The Court asked what the next step is. Mr. Wall stated that the major asset is the house which is vacant, that they would accept a neutral party and suggested Mr. Leutza who is present in court and that there must be a determination of testate or intestate. The Court stated that it does not want to take the matter off calendar and asked how much time they would need for litigation and could an accounting be done. Mr. Leow stated that his client is the successor trustee to Mr. Lagan, that she was not the trustee of the grandfather's trust and that mediation is a possibility. Mr. Wall stated that if a neutral is appointed it could expedite mediation. Mr. Loew stated that a neutral is a good idea, that they need to have an exchange of documents before mediation and that they need to do informal discovery. The Court stated that 120 should be appropriate to continue the underlying petition and continued the Petition for letters of administration to May 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that in the interim the parties are to identify a neutral for mediation and as administrator and that the trust issues need to be resolved first. Mr. Loew stated that they should be able to confer on a neutral administrator/mediator. Mr. Wall stated that they would like to avoid another action and that he worked on the estate plan. Mr. Brooks stated that a referral to mediation is appropriate. Mr. Loew stated that they need to exchange discovery before they can have a meaningful mediation. The Court stated that it would set a deadline prior to May 9th as follows: April 29, 2016 is the deadline for mediation and prior to that they are to exchange whatever information they have. The Court also stated that it does not know if it can weigh in without the trust issue resolved.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
